engineGuide.h2: Volkswagen Jetta Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volkswagen Jetta across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The CAYC 1.6 TDI became Jetta's most common diesel, balancing efficiency with reliability for fleet and private owners across Mk6.
compatibleVariants: S, SE, Sport, Highline, EfficientDynamics, BlueMotion
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey cars (60k-90k miles)
EGR cooler leak causing coolant loss
Injector seal failure leading to rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: CFFB 2.0 TDI powered the volume SE and GT trims, offering strong torque and motorway refinement for high-mileage drivers.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging reducing performance (50k-80k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking causing limp mode
Oil pump drive wear on early units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFFB Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output CFGB delivered 170hp for Sport and Highline trims, appealing to drivers wanting diesel performance without compromise.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear (70k-100k miles)
Turbo wastegate rattle under load
DPF regeneration issues in urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFGB Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: CLCA introduced EA189 emissions compliance to mid-cycle Mk6 Jetta, maintaining 140hp output with cleaner combustion.
commonFailures: EGR cooler leak (recalled on some units)
Swirl flap motor failure affecting throttle response
Injector carbon buildup causing misfire
entries.cta: Get quotes for CLCA Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: CBAB powered early Mk6 and late Mk5 Jetta diesels, a robust workhorse engine with proven longevity when maintained.
commonFailures: EGR system clogging reducing power (60k-90k miles)
Timing belt tensioner wear if not replaced on schedule
Oil pump drive collar wear on pre-2009 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBAB Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: BXE 1.9 TDI was the original Jetta diesel, renowned for durability but prone to unit injector failures at higher mileage.
commonFailures: Unit injector failure causing hard starting (80k-120k miles)
Turbo oil seal leak leading to blue smoke
Dual-mass flywear wear on manual transmissions
entries.cta: Get quotes for BXE Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: CAXA 1.4 TSI brought turbo efficiency to entry-level Jetta, but early units suffered timing chain tensioner issues before 2013 updates.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (40k-70k miles, pre-2013)
Carbon buildup on intake valves causing misfire
High-pressure fuel pump wear affecting cold starts
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAXA Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: CTHD twincharger (supercharger + turbo) delivered 160hp for Sport trims, offering strong performance but added complexity.
commonFailures: Supercharger clutch failure (50k-80k miles)
Turbo oil seal leak causing smoke under acceleration
Timing chain guide wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTHD Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: CCZB 2.0 TSI powered GT and performance trims, offering smooth power delivery but requiring timely timing chain maintenance.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner stretch (60k-90k miles)
PCV valve failure causing oil consumption
Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCZB Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: CCTA early 2.0 TSI powered pre-facelift GT models, a potent unit but with known HPFP and timing chain vulnerabilities.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure (50k-80k miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear requiring early replacement
Piston ring wear causing oil consumption on some units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCTA Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Jetta Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Pre-2013 Volkswagen Jetta 1.4 TSI Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Jetta S, SE, Sport 1.4 TSI (2006-2012, CAXA, CTHD, CCTA)
problems.rootCause: Early EA113/EA888 tensioners lacked sufficient hydraulic damping, allowing chain slack that can jump timing and cause catastrophic valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain before failure; requires engine front cover removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before rattling; prevents catastrophic damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace bent valves, retime engine, test compression; only if chain jumped but block intact
repairOptions.longevity: Risky; hidden damage may cause repeat failure; not recommended for high-mileage Jetta

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated tensioner, upgraded components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Jetta worth £2k-£6k; eliminates repeat failure risk; nationwide supply & fit available

problems.recommendation: If your Jetta 1.4 TSI has a rattling noise at cold start, don't delay - get a quote for a rebuilt engine with the updated tensioner fitted. For pre-2013 models under 60k miles with no symptoms, a preventative chain kit from a specialist may be cost-effective, but factor in labour costs that often approach replacement pricing.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAXA/CTHD engine prices for your Volkswagen Jetta

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Leak - Why Volkswagen Jetta TDI Engines Lose Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Jetta SE, GT, Highline 1.6/2.0 TDI (2010-2018, CAYC, CFFB, CFGB, CLCA)
problems.rootCause: Internal cracks in the EGR cooler allow exhaust gases to contaminate coolant, causing overheating, white smoke, and potential head gasket damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler assembly, flush cooling system, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before overheating damage; does not address underlying DPF/EGR system wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket, machine surface, reassemble; only if no warping
repairOptions.longevity: Risky on high-mileage TDI; hidden cracks may cause repeat failure; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TDI with updated EGR components, cleaned DPF system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Jetta with 80k+ miles; eliminates repeat EGR/DPF issues; supply & fit available UK-wide

problems.recommendation: If your Jetta TDI is losing coolant with no visible leak, get an EGR pressure test immediately. For vehicles over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with updated EGR components often costs less than dealer head gasket repair and provides long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAYC/CFFB Volkswagen Jetta diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: DPF Clogging & Oil Dilution - Why Volkswagen Jetta TDI Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Jetta S, SE, Sport 1.6/2.0 TDI (2010-2018, CAYC, CFFB, CLCA)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; unburnt fuel washes into sump, diluting oil and accelerating bearing wear, turbo damage, and eventual engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and chemically clean DPF, flush sump, replace oil/filter, reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before oil dilution damage; does not fix underlying EGR/boost issues causing clogging

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbo, bearings, seals; clean oil galleries; only if block not scored
repairOptions.longevity: Risky if oil dilution has caused bearing wear; hidden damage may cause repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TDI with cleaned DPF system, updated oil pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Jetta used for short trips; eliminates repeat DPF/oil dilution cycle; supply & fit available nationwide

problems.recommendation: If your Jetta TDI warning light is on and oil level is rising, stop driving immediately to avoid catastrophic failure. For urban-use Jettas over 70k miles, a rebuilt engine with DPF system inspection is often more cost-effective than repeated DPF cleans and turbo repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAYC/CFFB Volkswagen Jetta diesel engine prices

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Why Volkswagen Jetta Direct Injection Petrol Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Jetta S, SE, Sport 1.4/1.8/2.0 TSI (2006-2018, CAXA, CTHD, CCZB, CCTA)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection sprays fuel into cylinder, not over intake valves; oil vapour from PCV system bakes onto valves, restricting airflow and causing misfire, rough idle, and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, blast valves with walnut media, reassemble, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before severe buildup; requires repeat every 30k-40k miles; does not fix PCV source issue

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, clean intake system, update software; only if no valve damage
repairOptions.longevity: Helps delay recurrence but does not eliminate need for periodic cleaning on direct injection engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TSI with cleaned intake valves, updated PCV system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Jetta with recurring misfire; eliminates carbon-related failures; supply & fit available UK-wide

problems.recommendation: If your Jetta TSI has rough idle or misfire codes, get an intake inspection before assuming expensive repairs. For vehicles over 80k miles with recurring carbon issues, a rebuilt engine with cleaned components often costs less than repeated walnut blasts and provides long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAXA/CCZB Volkswagen Jetta petrol engine prices

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Why Volkswagen Jetta TSI Engines Struggle to Start
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Jetta GT, SE 2.0 TSI (2006-2012, CCTA, CCZB)
problems.rootCause: Early EA888 HPFP cam follower wears prematurely, causing metal debris to contaminate pump and injectors, leading to hard starting, misfire, and potential engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, cam follower, flush fuel system, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before injector contamination; does not address underlying cam lobe wear risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace contaminated injectors, clean fuel rails, test pressure; only if pump failure caught early
repairOptions.longevity: Risky if metal debris has entered cylinders; hidden damage may cause repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt TSI with updated HPFP components, cleaned fuel system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for Jetta GT/SE over 70k miles; eliminates repeat HPFP/injector failures; supply & fit available nationwide

problems.recommendation: If your Jetta 2.0 TSI has hard cold starts or fuel trim codes, get a HPFP pressure test immediately. For vehicles over 70k miles, a rebuilt engine with updated fuel components often costs less than dealer injector replacement and provides long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CCZB/CCTA Volkswagen Jetta 2.0 TSI engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Jetta
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain rattles to DPF clogging, Volkswagen Jetta engine failures are predictable and preventable with the right replacement strategy. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Jetta specialists, ensuring you get a rebuilt or reconditioned engine with upgraded components that address the original failure point. Every engine includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Jetta Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Jetta and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Jetta Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L TDI became the Jetta's most popular diesel across Mk6 (2010-2018), balancing fuel economy with adequate torque for fleet and private owners. CAYC and CAYB codes dominate this displacement, with DPF and EGR systems requiring attention on high-mileage or urban-use vehicles.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Jetta 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legacy 1.9L TDI powered early Mk5 Jetta (2005-2010), renowned for durability but using older unit injector technology prone to failure at higher mileage. BXE and BLS codes are most common, with timing belt maintenance critical for longevity.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Jetta 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI is the Jetta's performance diesel, available in 110hp, 140hp and 170hp outputs across Mk5 and Mk6. CFFB, CFGB, CLCA and CJAA codes cover EA189 and EA288 generations, with EGR, DPF and turbo systems requiring specialist attention.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Jetta 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TSI brought turbo efficiency to entry and mid-range Jetta trims, but pre-2013 units (CAXA, CTHD) suffered timing chain tensioner issues. Post-2013 codes (CHPA, CPTA) feature updated components and improved reliability for Sport and R-Line variants.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Jetta 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L TSI offered a mid-range petrol option for Jetta, bridging the gap between 1.4L efficiency and 2.0L performance. CDAA and BZB codes are most common, with similar timing chain and carbon buildup considerations as the 1.4L TSI family.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Jetta 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Jetta 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TSI powered GT and performance Jetta variants, delivering smooth power and strong torque. CCZB, CCTA and CBFA codes cover EA888 generations, with timing chain maintenance and HPFP reliability being key considerations for replacement decisions. Not sure which engine size your Volkswagen Jetta needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Volkswagen Jetta specialists.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Jetta 2.0L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Jetta Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Jetta's UK production timeline (2005-2018), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Mk5 Launch: BXE Diesel & Early TSI
years.description: The Jetta returned to the UK on the A5 platform, offering 1.9 TDI and early 1.4/2.0 TSI petrol engines with timing belt (diesel) or chain (petrol) drive.
keyChanges: Jetta relaunched in UK on A5 (PQ35) platform, saloon body style
1.9 TDI (BXE/BLS) and 2.0 TDI (CBAB) diesel options introduced
1.4 TSI (CAXA) and 2.0 TSI (CCTA) petrol engines with turbocharging
Manual and DSG automatic transmissions available across range
knownFor: Robust 1.9 TDI but unit injector failures at high mileage
Early 1.4/2.0 TSI timing chain tensioner vulnerability (pre-2013)
Strong fleet uptake of SE 2.0 TDI for company car use
DSG mechatronic issues on some early automatic models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2007 Volkswagen Jetta replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Jetta 1.9 TDI engine replacement • Volkswagen Jetta 1.4 TSI timing chain repair • Volkswagen Jetta 2.0 TDI EGR leak
enquiries: Volkswagen Jetta 1.4 TSI timing chain repair

years.preview: Mk5 Final Years: Tensioner Update Eligibility
years.description: Final Mk5 production years saw injector warranty extensions and timing chain update eligibility for pre-2013 TSI engines, critical for replacement decisions.
keyChanges: Injector warranty extension announced for 2007-2009 models (BXE/CBAB)
Timing chain tensioner update part number released for CAXA/CCTA
Minor trim updates; no major engine changes before Mk6 transition
knownFor: High-mileage BXE units prone to injector failure (80k-120k miles)
Pre-2013 CAXA/CCTA timing chain rattles at cold start
Strong residual values for well-maintained SE and GT trims
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Volkswagen Jetta replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Jetta injector warranty claim • Volkswagen Jetta timing chain update cost • Volkswagen Jetta 2.0 TDI DPF clean
enquiries: Volkswagen Jetta timing chain update cost

years.preview: Mk6 Launch: CAYC Diesel & Updated TSI
years.description: The Mk6 Jetta introduced the dominant CAYC 1.6 TDI and updated CFFB 2.0 TDI, while petrol TSI engines received revised tensioners from 2011 onwards.
keyChanges: Mk6 (A6 platform) launched with revised styling and interior
CAYC 1.6 TDI replaced 1.9 TDI as volume diesel option
CFFB 2.0 TDI 140hp became standard for SE/GT trims
Updated timing chain tensioner fitted to CAXA/CCZB from 2011
knownFor: CAYC 1.6 TDI became UK's most searched Jetta engine code
Post-2011 CAXA/CCZB have reduced timing chain failure risk
EGR cooler leak emerges as common CFFB/CFGB failure pattern
Strong fleet demand for SE 2.0 TDI with DSG automatic
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Volkswagen Jetta replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Jetta CAYC engine replacement • Volkswagen Jetta 1.4 TSI timing chain rattle • Volkswagen Jetta 2.0 TDI EGR cooler leak
enquiries: Volkswagen Jetta 1.4 TSI timing chain rattle
Volkswagen Jetta 2.0 TDI EGR cooler leak

years.preview: Mk6 Mid-Cycle: EA189 Compliance & AdBlue Introduction
years.description: Mid-cycle Mk6 updates introduced EA189 emissions compliance and AdBlue/SCR systems on late-model 2.0 TDI engines, affecting replacement component compatibility.
keyChanges: EA189 emissions update applied to CFFB/CFGB diesel engines
AdBlue/SCR system introduced on CJAA/CRBC 2.0 TDI from 2014
CTHD twincharger 1.4 TSI discontinued; CHPA single-turbo 1.4 TSI introduced
Minor trim updates; R-Line appearance pack added to range
knownFor: EA189 EGR recall affects some CFFB/CLCA units; updated parts in rebuilt engines
AdBlue system faults on CJAA/CRBC require specialist diagnosis
Post-2013 CHPA/CPTA have improved timing chain reliability vs pre-2013 CAXA
Highline and R-Line trims command premium for quality rebuilt engines
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Volkswagen Jetta replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Jetta EA189 EGR recall • Volkswagen Jetta AdBlue system fault • Volkswagen Jetta R-Line engine compatibility

years.preview: Mk6 Final Years: EA288 Dominance & End of Production
years.description: Final Jetta production years featured EA288 diesel engines with improved emissions systems, while petrol TSI codes remained largely unchanged before model discontinuation.
keyChanges: EA288 diesel engines (CJAA/CRBC) standard across 2.0 TDI range
No new engine codes introduced; focus on emissions compliance
Jetta discontinued in UK after 2018; parts availability remains strong
knownFor: EA288 engines have improved EGR/DPF reliability vs earlier EA189 units
Late-model CAYC/CJAA command higher rebuilt prices due to lower supply
Strong residual values for well-maintained Highline and R-Line trims
Parts availability remains excellent despite model discontinuation
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016-2018 Volkswagen Jetta replacement engines

faq.h2: Volkswagen Jetta Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Volkswagen Jetta engine typically costs £595-£2,150 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel 1.6 TDI (CAYC) is most affordable; petrol 2.0 TSI (CCZB) commands premium pricing.
keyPoints: Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier network
Supply & fit adds £350-£650 labour depending on location
All rebuilt engines include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Compare 3-5 quotes via EngineMarket for best value
items.cta: Compare live Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement prices UK

items.question: What engine codes fit my Volkswagen Jetta?
items.answer: Common Volkswagen Jetta engine codes include CAYC (1.6 TDI), CFFB/CFGB (2.0 TDI), CAXA/CTHD (1.4 TSI) and CCZB (2.0 TSI). Enter your reg or engine code for exact compatibility verification.
keyPoints: Diesel: CAYC, CFFB, CFGB, CLCA, CJAA, BXE
Petrol: CAXA, CTHD, CCZB, CCTA, CHPA
Use DVLA lookup or engine code sticker for verification
EngineMarket specialists confirm compatibility before dispatch
items.cta: Verify your Volkswagen Jetta engine code and get instant quotes

items.question: Is a reconditioned Volkswagen Jetta engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - reconditioned Volkswagen Jetta engines from EngineMarket specialists undergo strip, clean, test and rebuild with worn parts replaced, backed by minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Bench-tested before dispatch to ensure performance meets spec
Upgraded components address known failure points (timing chain, EGR)
Warranty terms confirmed with chosen supplier before order
Ideal for Jetta owners seeking dealer-quality reliability at lower cost
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Volkswagen Jetta engine prices with warranty

items.question: How long does Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Volkswagen Jetta engines typically dispatch in 3-5 working days; supply & fit appointments take 1-2 days depending on workshop availability and engine complexity.
keyPoints: Used engines may dispatch faster; rebuilt units require build time
Supply & fit includes removal, fitting, fluids and road test
Nationwide network of approved fitters minimises wait times
Book early for urgent replacements to secure preferred slot

items.question: Can I keep my Volkswagen Jetta DSG with a replacement engine?
items.answer: Yes - replacement Volkswagen Jetta engines are compatible with DSG transmissions; the mechatronic unit is separate and not included in engine supply, but compatibility is assured for your variant.
keyPoints: Engine code is primary compatibility factor for DSG models
Mechatronic unit inspected separately if transmission issues suspected
Supply & fit specialists confirm DSG compatibility before fitting
DSG fluid change recommended during engine replacement service
items.cta: Compare DSG-compatible Volkswagen Jetta engine replacement prices

items.question: What causes Volkswagen Jetta timing chain failure?
items.answer: Pre-2013 Volkswagen Jetta 1.4/2.0 TSI engines (CAXA, CCTA, CCZB) suffered timing chain tensioner design flaws allowing chain slack, potentially jumping timing and causing valve damage.
keyPoints: Rattling noise at cold start is key warning symptom
Updated tensioner part numbers available for rebuilt engines
Class action settlement covered some pre-2013 models; check eligibility
Rebuilt engines include updated tensioner to prevent repeat failure
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Volkswagen Jetta TSI engines with updated tensioner

items.question: Is it worth replacing a high-mileage Volkswagen Jetta engine?
items.answer: Yes - if your Volkswagen Jetta chassis is sound, a rebuilt engine (£595-£2,150) often costs less than dealer repair and extends vehicle life cost-effectively versus selling or scrapping.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost vs Jetta's current market value
Rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing original failures
Supply & fit available nationwide for convenience
Minimum 12-month warranty provides peace of mind post-replacement
items.cta: Calculate if your high-mileage Volkswagen Jetta is worth engine replacement

items.question: How do I find my Volkswagen Jetta engine code?
items.answer: Your Volkswagen Jetta engine code appears on a sticker in the timing cover area, service book, or V5C document; enter your reg on EngineMarket for instant DVLA verification.
keyPoints: Common codes: CAYC (1.6 TDI), CFFB (2.0 TDI), CAXA (1.4 TSI)
Engine code sticker location: timing cover, boot floor, service stamp
DVLA lookup via registration provides instant code confirmation
Specialists verify code before dispatch to ensure compatibility
